_comment: Harness-neutral CORE enforcement descriptor (opencode-harness-support, stage
(a), slice 3). Names — once, neutrally — the protocol's load-bearing CORE
deny rules so each harness's manifest realizes them in that harness's
primitive. The generator reads this to emit the OpenCode enforcement plugin
(.opencode/plugin/<name>.js); the Claude side realizes the same rules as the
PreToolUse hooks already authored verbatim in
src/manifests/claude/settings.json.
SINGLE-SOURCE of the wb-* role set: this file does NOT re-list the wb-* role
names. The ONE authored copy of that set lives in the Claude settings.json
Task|Agent|Skill matcher (its escaped shell case-pattern is the verbatim
single home — reproducing those exact bytes from a neutral source is risky).
The generator EXTRACTS the role set from settings.json at build time and
injects it into the OpenCode plugin, so there is exactly one authored copy of
the role set and the two adapters cannot drift. The role_set_source field
below records where that single copy lives; the single-source test asserts
the emitted plugin's role set equals what settings.json declares.
SCOPE — CORE rules only. The 'ask'-class guards in settings.json (force-push,
git commit --no-verify, ssh content-edit, ssh mutating action) are OUT OF
SCOPE here: OpenCode's tool.execute.before can only throw (hard deny) or
allow — it has no 'ask' return — so those map to OpenCode's permission
config, not this plugin (documented divergence; AGENTS.md records it). Only
the clear-DENY guards below are ported.

role_set_source.note: The single authored copy of the wb-* role-duplicator set. The generator extracts the case-pattern role list from this matcher's command and injects it into the OpenCode plugin. No second hand-maintained list exists.

rules.neutral: Block spawning a wb-* role-duplicator subagent (the role set is single-sourced from settings.json).
Block loading a wb-* role-duplicator skill (mirror the Claude skill deny path).
Block a read whose target path resolves outside the project root.
Block an empty/whitespace-only write over an existing non-empty file (truncation guard).
